subject: Cloud Computing
title: PaaS Lock-In Risks
description: Learn PaaS Lock-In Risks in one minute.

hook.text: You built your entire app on a PaaS. Now the provider raises prices 300%. Moving is nearly impossible.

buildup.text: PaaS simplifies by hiding infrastructure. But that means your app depends on proprietary platform features.

discovery.text: Custom APIs, build systems, and integrations tie you to one provider. Migration means rewriting code.

twist.text: Some teams accept lock-in willingly. The speed of shipping outweighs the risk of vendor dependency.

climax.text: The best protection is building on open standards — containers, SQL, standard protocols.

punchline.text: PaaS is fast to adopt. It can be painful to leave.

quiz.question: What causes PaaS lock-in?
options: Dependence on proprietary platform features that don't exist elsewhere
PaaS providers legally prevent customers from leaving
Applications deployed on PaaS cannot be backed up
